name: vscode-issues-as-code
description: Synchronize GitHub issues to a local .issues/ folder

commands.title: Issues as Code: Add Sync Target

issuesAsCode.autoPush.description: Controls when local changes are automatically pushed to the remote. 'afterDelay' pushes after the configured delay. 'onFocusChange' pushes when the editor loses focus. 'onWindowChange' pushes when the VS Code window loses focus. 'off' disables auto-push entirely. Manual saves (Ctrl+S) always push immediately regardless of this setting.

issuesAsCode.autoPushDelay.description: Milliseconds to wait after last save before pushing to the remote. Only used when autoPush is set to 'afterDelay'.

issuesAsCode.syncTargets.description: List of sync targets. Each entry defines a local folder and one or more plugin configurations (e.g. gh-issues). When empty and a GitHub remote is detected, the extension automatically adds a default open-issues target to workspace settings.

filesDir.description: Local folder path where synced files are stored. Must be relative to the current workspace folder; absolute paths are not allowed.

naming.description: Template for file names. Use tokens like {gh-issues.number} and {gh-issues.title}.

readOnly.description: When true, files are updated from remote but local changes are never pushed. Local edits are overwritten on each pull. Files are made read-only on disk.

label.description: Filter by one or more labels.

created_at.description: Filter by creation date, e.g. '>2025-01-01'. Supports {today-Nd} tokens.

issuesAsCode.autoFetchInterval.description: Minutes between automatic fetches from the remote. Fetching checks for remote changes and stores them locally without applying to task files.

issuesAsCode.autoPullOnFetch.description: When enabled, automatically applies remote changes to local task files after fetching, as long as there are no local modifications that would conflict.

issuesAsCode.syncStatePath.description: Path to the local sync state file that tracks the last-synced timestamp per issue. Must be relative to the current workspace folder; absolute paths are not allowed. This file is machine-local and should be gitignored.

issuesAsCode.showSyncState.description: Show the sync state file in the VS Code Explorer. When false (default), the file is hidden via files.exclude.

issuesAsCode.showSyncIcons.description: Controls which sync status icons are shown on issue files in the Explorer.

newIssue.description: Show 'A' badge on new issues not yet pushed to remote.

modified.description: Show 'M' badge on locally modified issues not yet pushed to remote.

synchronized.description: Show '✓' badge on issues whose local copy matches the remote.

issuesAsCode.enable_experimental_project.description: Enable experimental GitHub Projects v2 metadata sync. Requires reloading VS Code to take effect.

issuesAsCode.rateLimitThreshold.description: Percentage of API quota remaining that triggers an automatic sync pause. When the remaining quota drops to or below this threshold, automatic syncing is paused until the quota resets. Manual actions (Sync Now, Publish) are still allowed with a confirmation prompt.

issuesAsCode.showStatusBarIcon.description: Show the Issues as Code status bar icon. When enabled, a cloud icon is displayed in the status bar showing sync summary and API quota information on hover.

scripts.test:unit: mocha --ui tdd out/test/fileManager.test.js out/test/configManager.test.js out/test/syncManager.test.js out/test/syncStateManager.test.js out/test/issueDecorationProvider.test.js out/test/publishCodeLensProvider.test.js out/test/frontmatterCompletionProvider.test.js out/test/rateLimitMonitor.test.js out/test/statusBarManager.test.js
